pulse: The financial sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 7.8x and a P/E of 14.7x, which aligns closely with the broader market's valuation metrics. Notably, Goldman Sachs (GS) is demonstrating strong performance with a trailing P/E of 16.3x and a revenue growth rate of 15.2%, reflecting its strategic focus on precision and adaptability highlighted in McKinsey's latest review of the banking landscape. As rate cuts favor a resurgence in bank performance, investors may find appealing opportunities in stocks like Bank of America (BAC) and Wells Fargo (WFC), which are well-positioned to capitalize on a changing interest rate environment.
